Deputy Todd Widman Memorial Highway Last Call 3-1-2000

honoree.name: Todd Widman
honoree.summary: Deputy Todd Widman was shot and killed after responding to a call of a transient walking along U.S. Highway 36. Upon arrival, Deputy Widman was unable to locate the transient. Approximately one hour later, he observed a person matching the description and began questioning him. He was transporting the subject back to the department when he was shot. Deputy Widman was transported to a local hospital where he died of the gunshot wounds. The juvenile killer fled the scene but was shot and killed after exchanging gunfire with officers about an hour later. Deputy Widman had served with the Brown County Sheriff's Department for two years. He is survived by his mother and sister.
honoree.incidentDescription: Cause: Gunfire Weapon: Handgun Offender: Shot and killed by other officers

honoree.name: Robert B. Docking
honoree.summary: Robert Docking was an American politician who served as the 37th Governor of Kansas from 1967 to 1975. He was born on April 10, 1925, in Arkansas City, Kansas, and died on March 26, 1983, in Kansas City, Missouri. A member of the Democratic Party, he was known for his fiscal conservatism and support for education. His tenure as governor was marked by steady economic growth and infrastructure development in the state.

honoree.name: Martin Luther King Jr
honoree.summary: Martin Luther King Jr. was born on January 15, 1929, in Atlanta, Georgia, and was a Baptist minister and civil rights leader. He led the American civil rights movement from the mid-1950s until his assassination in 1968, advocating for racial equality through nonviolent protest. King delivered his iconic 'I Have a Dream' speech during the 1963 March on Washington and won the Nobel Peace Prize in 1964. His work and legacy continue to influence social justice movements worldwide.
honoree.education: Morehouse College, Crozer Theological Seminary, Boston University

honoree.name: The Dr. Martin Luther King Jr
honoree.summary: Martin Luther King Jr. was born on January 15, 1929, in Atlanta, Georgia, and was assassinated on April 4, 1968, in Memphis, Tennessee. He was a Baptist minister and the most prominent leader in the American civil rights movement from 1955 until his death. King advanced civil rights through nonviolence and civil disobedience, inspired by his Christian beliefs and the nonviolent activism of Mahatma Gandhi. He is historically significant for his role in the Montgomery Bus Boycott, the March on Washington, and helping to secure the passage of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and the Voting Rights Act of 1965.
honoree.education: Morehouse College, Crozer Theological Seminary, Boston University

honoree.name: Pack St. Clair
honoree.summary: Lesley J. McNair was a United States Army officer who served from 1904 until his death in 1944. He was a key planner in the development of American military doctrine and played a significant role in training troops for World War II. McNair was killed by friendly fire in Normandy shortly after the D-Day landings. He is remembered for his substantial contributions to the Army's operational strategies and training programs.

highways.name: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Highway

honoree.name: Martin Luther King Jr
honoree.summary: Martin Luther King Jr. was born on January 15, 1929, in Atlanta, Georgia, and was a Baptist minister and civil rights leader. He led the American civil rights movement from the mid-1950s until his assassination in 1968, advocating for racial equality through nonviolent protest. King delivered his iconic 'I Have a Dream' speech during the 1963 March on Washington and won the Nobel Peace Prize in 1964. His work and legacy continue to influence social justice movements worldwide.

highways.name: Master Trooper Dean A. Goodheart Memorial Highway

honoree.name: Master Trooper Dean A. Goodheart
honoree.summary: Trooper Dean Goodheart was killed after being struck by a car on I-70, near Oakley, while performing a routine safety inspection. On May 16, 2000, a bill known as the Goodheart Law requiring drivers on multi-lane roads to slow down or move over was signed into law by the Kansas governor. Trooper Goodheart had served with the Kansas Highway Patrol for 23 years. He was survived by his wife, daughter, two sons, mother, and three brothers.
